organic_results.title: FDA Updates and Press Announcements on NDMA in Metformin
organic_results.snippet: FDA has been investigating the presence of N-Nitrosodimethylamine (NDMA), a probable human carcinogen, in metformin products. Several lots have been voluntarily recalled.

organic_results.title: Apotex Recalls Metformin HCl Extended-Release Tablets — Lots APX5523, APX5524
organic_results.snippet: Apotex Corp. is voluntarily recalling specific lots of metformin HCl extended-release tablets 500mg due to NDMA above the FDA interim acceptable intake limit of 96 ng/day.

organic_results.title: FAERS Public Dashboard — Metformin Adverse Events
organic_results.snippet: Search FAERS for adverse events associated with Metformin. Over 81,000 reports submitted between 2012 and 2024.

organic_results.title: EMA — Metformin-containing medicines: NDMA review concluded
organic_results.snippet: The European Medicines Agency's PRAC reviewed NDMA contamination in metformin and recommended manufacturers test product and confirm levels remain below interim limits.

organic_results.title: Metformin and NDMA: A systematic review of nitrosamine contamination — PubMed
organic_results.snippet: Systematic review of NDMA detection methodology, regulatory action timelines, and patient outcomes following metformin recalls. PMID: 35142345.

organic_results.title: Apotex Investor Press Release — Voluntary Recall Statement
organic_results.snippet: Apotex statement on voluntary recall: 'Out of an abundance of caution, we are pulling specific lots while we work with FDA to confirm root cause. Patient safety remains our priority.'

organic_results.title: FDA Updates and Press Announcements on Angiotensin II Receptor Blocker (ARB) Recalls (Valsartan)
organic_results.snippet: FDA's ongoing investigation of nitrosamines in ARB products. Multiple manufacturers have voluntarily recalled valsartan-containing products.

organic_results.title: FAERS — Valsartan adverse events dashboard
organic_results.snippet: FAERS adverse event reports for Valsartan — over 18,000 reports since 2018.
